summary: Matched two small Dolphin library units by replacing assembly source files for PPCArch and AmcExi2Stubs with C sources and headers, updating obj_files.mk to build the new C objects, and propagating newly identified PPCArch symbol names into existing assembly call sites and include/functions.h. The PR primarily converted trivial EXI stub routines and low-level PowerPC register helper routines into source while improving symbol names such as PPCMtmsr, PPCMfhid0, PPCMtdec, PPCSync, and PPCMtwpar.
what_changed: Deleted asm/dolphin/amcstubs/AmcExi2Stubs.s and added src/dolphin/amcstubs/AmcExi2Stubs.c with equivalent trivial C implementations: EXI2_Init, EXI2_EnableInterrupts, EXI2_Reserve, and EXI2_Unreserve return immediately; EXI2_Poll, EXI2_ReadN, and EXI2_WriteN return FALSE; AMC_IsStub returns TRUE.
Added src/dolphin/amcstubs/AmcExi2Stubs.h with prototypes for the EXI2 and AMC stub functions and an include of dolphin/types.h for BOOL.
Deleted asm/dolphin/base/PPCArch.s and added src/dolphin/base/PPCArch.c using inline asm functions with nofralloc for low-level PowerPC operations.
Added src/dolphin/base/PPCArch.h with prototypes for PPCMfmsr, PPCMtmsr, PPCMfhid0, PPCMfl2cr, PPCMtl2cr, PPCMtdec, PPCSync, PPCHalt, PPCMfhid2, PPCMthid2, and PPCMtwpar.
Updated obj_files.mk to build src/dolphin/amcstubs/AmcExi2Stubs.o and src/dolphin/base/PPCArch.o instead of their asm object paths.

Renamed unresolved PPCArch function references in include/functions.h from address-based names to apparent SDK-style names: func_80335E64 to PPCMtmsr, func_80335E6C to PPCMfhid0, func_80335E84 to PPCMtdec, func_80335E8C to PPCSync, and func_80335EB8 to PPCMtwpar.

Updated assembly call-site comments/labels in OSAlarm.s, OSCache.s, GXFifo.s, GXInit.s, and melee/db/code_80225374.s to reference the newly named PPCArch functions while preserving the same branch instruction encodings and addresses.
smart_moves: Used inline asm with nofralloc for PPCArch helpers, which is appropriate for one- or two-instruction leaf routines that must preserve exact register use and avoid compiler-generated prologue/epilogue code.
Converted AmcExi2Stubs to simple C rather than inline asm because the original assembly was just blr or constant return stubs, making C sufficient and much easier to maintain.
Renamed call sites across still-assembly files after matching PPCArch, improving future readability without needing to decompile the callers immediately.
Kept build integration explicit in obj_files.mk by swapping only the two matched object paths from asm to src, minimizing risk to unrelated object ordering.
Introduced headers alongside new source files so later decompilation work can include named prototypes instead of relying solely on include/functions.h comments.
decomp_lessons: Very small SDK/library stubs are good early asm-to-C targets: if the function is a bare return or constant BOOL return, a direct C implementation can match cleanly and reduce assembly surface area.
For hardware register helpers, matching may require inline asm rather than C expressions because instructions like mfmsr, mtmsr, mfspr, mtspr, sc, and sync have exact opcode requirements.

When replacing assembly with source, update both the object list and all known symbol references; otherwise the code may build but the knowledge graph and future decompilation context remain address-based.
A matched low-level utility file can unlock better names in many unrelated systems, as seen with OSAlarm, OSCache, GX FIFO/init, and debug code call sites.
Use header files to capture confirmed function names and signatures as soon as asm is promoted to source, but avoid overclaiming parameter types when evidence only shows empty prototypes.
naming_conventions: Dolphin PowerPC architecture helpers use the PPC prefix followed by the instruction/register operation, e.g. PPCMfmsr, PPCMtmsr, PPCMfhid0, PPCMfl2cr, PPCMtl2cr, PPCMtdec, PPCMfhid2, PPCMthid2, and PPCMtwpar.
Stubbed EXI2 functions use uppercase SDK-style names with the EXI2_ prefix: EXI2_Init, EXI2_EnableInterrupts, EXI2_Poll, EXI2_ReadN, EXI2_WriteN, EXI2_Reserve, and EXI2_Unreserve.
The AMC stub indicator uses the uppercase SDK-style name AMC_IsStub.
Header guards in this PR use uppercase underscore-wrapped file names, such as _PPCARCH_H_ and _AMCEXI2STUBS_H_.
Unknown address-based names like func_80335E64 were replaced once the operation was identified, but prototypes still omitted parameter lists/types where the diff did not establish them.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used Metrowerks-style `asm void` functions with `nofralloc` to emit exact leaf routines for special-purpose PowerPC instructions.
Mapped `mfmsr r3; blr` to PPCMfmsr and `mtmsr r3; blr` to PPCMtmsr.

Mapped SPR accesses to named helpers: `mfspr r3, 0x3f0` to PPCMfhid0, `mfspr r3, 0x3f9` to PPCMfl2cr, `mtspr 0x3f9, r3` to PPCMtl2cr, `mtspr 0x16, r3` to PPCMtdec, `mfspr r3, 0x398` to PPCMfhid2, `mtspr 0x398, r3` to PPCMthid2, and `mtspr 0x399, r3` to PPCMtwpar.
Recreated PPCHalt as inline asm with a local `halt_loop` label containing sync, nops, li r3,0, and a self-branch, matching the original infinite halt loop shape.
For AmcExi2Stubs, relied on C constant returns FALSE/TRUE to match simple `li r3, 0/1; blr` assembly.
Assembly files outside the matched units were not decompiled; only their branch target labels were renamed to the new PPCArch symbols.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that add typed parameter lists for PPCArch.h or AmcExi2Stubs.h prototypes.
Search for remaining func_80335E-prefixed symbols to see whether other PPCArch or DB functions were later identified.
Check whether PPCSync is the intended SDK name for the `sc; blr` helper or if later work corrected this naming.
Find subsequent decompilation of OSAlarm or OSCache that includes PPCMtdec, PPCMfhid0, or PPCMtmsr calls in C.
